evals.prompt: Build me a card component for a SaaS dashboard. Use a clean, modern style.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT default to Inter, Roboto, or system fonts. Should use tinted near-black/near-white instead of pure #000/#fff. Should use layered shadows, not a single box-shadow.
expectations: Does not use Inter, Roboto, Arial, or system-ui as the font choice
Does not use pure #000 or #fff — uses tinted near-black/near-white instead
Uses layered (multi-value) box-shadow for depth, not a single shadow
Defines colors as CSS custom properties or design tokens, not hardcoded hex in the component
Does not use bounce or elastic easing

evals.prompt: I have a Next.js app with Tailwind v4 and a components/ui/ directory with existing Button and Input components from shadcn. I need a new DataTable component. How should I approach this?
evals.expected_output: Should check for and reuse the existing shared UI library (shadcn components) rather than building from scratch. Should follow the project's existing component patterns.
expectations: Checks for existing shared UI library before creating new base components
Reuses existing shadcn Button and Input components rather than creating new ones
Places the component in the project's component directory structure
Mentions text truncation in tables requires a wrapper div with truncate class (td ignores overflow: hidden)
Respects the RSC boundary — only adds 'use client' if the component needs interactivity

evals.prompt: My app has a main content area that sometimes overflows and sometimes doesn't. When it switches between overflowing and not, the layout shifts because the scrollbar appears/disappears. How should I fix this?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend overflow-y: scroll on the main container with custom thin scrollbar styling, NOT scrollbar-gutter: stable.
expectations: Recommends overflow-y: scroll to always reserve scrollbar space
Does NOT recommend scrollbar-gutter: stable (conflicts with custom thin scrollbar overrides)
Suggests custom CSS for invisible scrollbar that reveals on hover
Mentions the width mismatch issue between browser default scrollbar and custom 6px width

evals.prompt: Build a media gallery where clicking thumbnails opens a lightbox with the full-size image. The user can tap different thumbnails in quick succession, and I want smooth morphing transitions from thumbnail to full screen, with the backdrop darkening in sync. I'm on React; performance matters — I'm targeting 60fps. Build the complete implementation.
evals.expected_output: Production-grade shared-element implementation preferring the native View Transitions API: unique per-image transition names, cleanup of the source name inside the transition callback, backdrop paired with the image on identical easing/duration, and customized ::view-transition-group timing instead of the default crossfade.
expectations: The image morphs between states using the browser's native view-transition mechanism rather than a JS animation library, so the transition runs compositor-side for 60fps
Each thumbnail-to-lightbox transition gets a distinct identifier derived from the image id, so different images can transition without name collisions
The source element's transition name is cleared inside the startViewTransition callback before the target receives it, preventing stale-name collisions when transitions repeat
The backdrop and the image animate with the same easing curve and duration so they read as one coordinated motion, not two independent effects
Transition timing is customized via ::view-transition-group (or equivalent pseudo-element styling) rather than relying on the default crossfade

evals.prompt: I'm building a dropdown menu that appears when the user clicks a button. What easing and duration should I use for the enter and exit animations?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend ease-out for both enter and exit (user-initiated interaction), duration 150-250ms for standard UI, exit ~20% faster than entrance. Should pair the dropdown and any overlay with the same easing and duration.
expectations: Recommends ease-out for the dropdown entrance (not ease-in or ease-in-out)
Recommends ease-out for exit as well (element leaving the screen)
Duration is in the 150-250ms range for standard UI elements
Exit animation is faster than entrance (approximately 20% shorter)
Paired elements rule: overlay/backdrop uses same easing and duration as the dropdown

evals.prompt: I'm building a complex animated landing page section with a card that fades in, a heading that highlights, detail rows that stagger in, and a CTA button that appears last. How should I structure the animation code?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend the storyboard pattern: ASCII storyboard comment, TIMING object with absolute ms values, element config objects (UPPERCASE), stage-driven sequencing with a single stage integer state.
expectations: Recommends an ASCII storyboard comment at the top with right-aligned ms values
Uses a single TIMING object with absolute ms values (not deltas between stages)
Element configs are UPPERCASE named objects (e.g., CARD, ROWS) grouping all values for one element
Uses a single stage integer state with setTimeout chain, not scattered boolean flags
JSX uses stage >= N checks for progressive reveal

evals.prompt: Build a dashboard page with 6 collapsible card sections (each expands/collapses on click to reveal more data). The page loads with a staggered fade-in. Users frequently navigate from here to a detail page that takes 200-300ms to load, and I want that navigation to feel instant. I'm on React + Framer Motion. Implement the height animation on expand, the reduced-motion support, and the prefetch strategy.
evals.expected_output: Production-grade implementation showing the container-animation lifecycle (ResizeObserver via callback ref, zero-bounds guard, overflow clipping), a complete reduced-motion opt-out, and intent-based prefetching beyond simple hover.
expectations: Container height animates smoothly on expand/collapse using ResizeObserver to measure content (not re-measuring with getBoundingClientRect on every render), avoiding layout thrashing
The measurement hook attaches via a callback ref so the observer connects exactly when the node mounts, never missing the initial measurement
Guards the initial zero-bounds measurement by falling back to 'auto' until a positive size is observed, so nothing visibly pops open on mount
Content clips cleanly during the height transition (overflow hidden on the animating container) instead of spilling outside mid-animation
Reduced-motion support disables the animations entirely (media query or hook) — instant state changes, not merely shorter durations
The detail-page link prefetches on predicted intent (cursor trajectory / expanded intent area), not just onMouseEnter, so the 200-300ms load is hidden before the click

evals.prompt: Before I start building, I need to understand the project's design contract and component inventory. My project has a VISION.md at the root with defined animation timing rules and a COMPONENTS.md file generated by npx design-fleet inventory that documents existing reusable components (buttons, modals, data tables, forms). I'm about to build a new expandable form section component and a drag-to-dismiss card animation. Walk me through your complete workflow before writing any code — what do you check first, how do you handle the reuse gate, what does the motion timing layer expect, and what contract rules would override generic guidance?
evals.expected_output: Demonstrates the contract-first workflow: VISION.md read as the binding contract (its timing table overrides generic durations), COMPONENTS.md searched by problem before building, reuse-or-evolve-in-place over forking, spring physics for the gesture-driven dismissal, and a complete reduced-motion opt-out for both animations.
expectations: Starts by reading VISION.md and treats it as the binding project contract that overrides both the taste profile and generic skill guidance
Uses the animation-timing table from VISION.md for durations instead of generic defaults, when the table exists
Searches COMPONENTS.md by the problem being solved (expandable sections, drag interactions) rather than by a component's visual name
Reuses or extends an existing component in place rather than forking a near-duplicate, and only builds new after finding nothing that covers the need
Identifies drag-to-dismiss as gesture-driven motion requiring spring physics with velocity passthrough, not a fixed CSS easing curve
Requires a complete reduced-motion opt-out for both animations (disabled entirely, not shortened)

evals.prompt: I want to add a decorative line under my heading using CSS. Should I add an empty <span> element or use a pseudo-element? And what CSS properties are required?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend ::after pseudo-element over an extra DOM node. Must mention content: '' as required, and position: relative on the parent for absolute positioning.
expectations: Recommends ::after pseudo-element instead of an extra DOM node
Specifies that content: '' (or content: "") is required for the pseudo-element to render
Requires position: relative on the parent element for absolute pseudo-element positioning
Mentions z-index layering if the pseudo-element needs to sit behind text

evals.prompt: I need to animate a container's height when its content changes (like an accordion opening/closing). How should I structure the React component?
evals.expected_output: Should describe the two-div pattern: outer motion.div animates the size, inner div holds the measurement ref. Should use ResizeObserver, not getBoundingClientRect. Should guard initial bounds of 0.
expectations: Describes the two-div pattern: outer animated div and inner measured div, never the same element
Uses ResizeObserver for measurement, not getBoundingClientRect on every render
Guards against initial bounds of 0 by falling back to 'auto' until bounds are positive
Sets overflow: hidden on the animated container during transitions
Uses a callback ref (not useRef) for reliable ResizeObserver lifecycle

evals.prompt: I want to prefetch the next page when the user's cursor moves toward a link, before they actually hover over it. What pattern should I use?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend trajectory-based prediction (like ForesightJS) over simple onMouseEnter prefetch, which reclaims 100-200ms. Should mention hitSlop for earlier prediction and touch device fallback.
expectations: Recommends trajectory prediction over simple hover/onMouseEnter prefetch
Mentions the 100-200ms advantage that trajectory prediction reclaims
Recommends hitSlop to trigger predictions earlier (larger hit area)
Includes touch device fallback since there's no cursor on touch devices
Advises selective use: only where navigation latency is noticeable, not on every link

evals.prompt: I want to add a page transition animation between routes in my Next.js app. Should I use Framer Motion's layoutId/AnimatePresence or the View Transitions API?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend the View Transitions API over JS library approaches for page transitions. Should mention view-transition-name as required, names must be unique during transition, and cleanup after completion.
expectations: Recommends View Transitions API over JS library (Framer Motion layoutId) for page transitions
Specifies that view-transition-name is required on participating elements
Each transition name must be unique during the transition
Transition names should be cleaned up after completion
Mentions styling via ::view-transition-group pseudo-elements for custom animation
